units.title: Greetings
units.description: Say hello, goodbye, please and thank you

units.title: Travel Phrases
units.description: Where, how much, I want, help

units.title: Body & Feelings
units.description: Head, hand, hungry, tired, happy, sad

units.title: Weather & Nature
units.description: Sun, rain, cold, hot, sky, water

units.title: Getting Around
units.description: Car, bus, train, left, right, here, there

frameworks.title: Word order: SOV
frameworks.body: Urdu places the verb at the END. 'I water drink' instead of 'I drink water'.

frameworks.title: Postpositions
frameworks.body: Where English says 'to the house', Urdu says 'house to' (گھر کو).

frameworks.title: Gender matters
frameworks.body: Every noun is masculine or feminine, and verbs change to match.

frameworks.title: Honour shapes pronouns
frameworks.body: آپ formal, تم familiar, تو intimate. Use تو with a stranger and you offend.

examples.native: میں جاؤں گا لیکن دیر سے

alphabetGroups.title: Starter letters
alphabetGroups.description: Alif, Bay, Pay, Tay — the most common starting sounds

alphabetGroups.title: Common consonants
alphabetGroups.description: Jeem, Chay, Daal, Ray, Seen, Sheen

alphabetGroups.title: Tricky throat sounds
alphabetGroups.description: Hay, Khay, Ayn, Qaaf — sounds English doesn't have

alphabetGroups.title: The rest
alphabetGroups.description: Fay, Kaaf, Gaaf, Laam, Meem, Noon, Wao, Hay, Yay
